_comment: Windows SDK installer URL catalog for the crashdumps module. We only need the Debugging Tools for Windows (WinDbg / kd) — installed as a component of the full SDK. Microsoft's fwlink IDs rot; verify monthly. verifyMonthly: true means the diagnose step should print a reminder when verifiedDate is > 30 days old. Try primary URL first; on non-200 or failed install, fall back through the fallback array in order.
_installComponent: OptionId.WindowsDesktopDebuggers is the only component we need — a ~200MB install versus a multi-GB full SDK.
refreshInstructions: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/debugger/ — pull the current Windows SDK download link from the 'Debugging Tools for Windows' page. Also check winget: winget search Microsoft.WindowsSDK.

primary.verifiedSource: knowledge_base/session_2026-07-04_lenovo_slim7prox.md — installed and used successfully to !analyze -v the RTL8822CE minidumps

fallback.note: winget silently installs the same package. Slower than the fwlink direct download but survives fwlink rot.
fallback.verify: Package ID — may become Microsoft.WindowsSDK.10.0.<newer> in the future

fallback.note: Newest available WindowsSDK regardless of version — use if the pinned version ID is retired.
Chocolatey has an older standalone WinDbg package. Last resort; verify current version before use.
fallback.verify: Choco package name — the version-2004 suffix is stale
